content.text:
Based on the image, I can analyze each state's change from April to June using the y-axis scales:

- **Texas**: Y-axis up to 2000, rising from ~300 in April to ~2000 in June (~1700 increase)
- **Arizona**: Y-axis up to 1000, rising from ~200 to ~1000 (~800 increase)
- **North Carolina**: Y-axis up to 600, rising from ~200 to ~600 (~400 increase)
- **South Carolina**: Y-axis up to 400, rising from ~0 to ~400 (~400 increase)
- Other states show smaller absolute changes

<think>
The subtitle notes scales are adjusted per state. In absolute terms, Texas has by far the largest scale (0–2000) and shows hospitalizations rising from roughly 300 to ~2000 from April to June — an increase of approximately 1700. Arizona is second at ~800. Texas clearly has the highest absolute change.
</think>

<answer>
Texas has the highest change in coronavirus hospitalizations from April to June, rising from approximately 300 to ~2000 (an increase of roughly 1,700 hospitalizations), which is the largest absolute change among all states shown.
</answer>
